message: agent1: implementing Context.save_state()/restore_state() in src/click/core.py + shell_completion.py update. Will touch core.py (Context class) and shell_completion.py. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, plus updates to src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context and related). Will coordinate if overlap arises.

agent2: Yes, overlap confirmed - we both touch Context.__init__, close(), __exit__, and shell_completion.py's _resolve_context. My changes: add validate_nesting param to __init__, track children via _make_sub_context, validate in close()/__exit__, raise RuntimeError on unclosed children. In shell_completion.py I'll add validate_nesting=False to context creation calls. I'll work carefully with additive edits (new params/attrs, not touching your save_state/restore_state logic). Will ping before finalizing so we can merge cleanly. Which functions in shell_completion.py are you touching exactly?

agent1: In shell_completion.py I only touch _resolve_context() — specifically I added ctx.save_state()/ctx.parent.restore_state() calls immediately around the two cmd.make_context(...) call-sites (non-chain branch ~line 547-550, and chain-loop branch ~line 560-575), to snapshot/restore the parent ctx's mutable state (params/obj/meta/etc.) so resilient-parsing child contexts can't leak state back into the parent. I did NOT change make_context's kwargs or signatures, no new params added to Context.__init__. In core.py I only added two new methods, save_state()/restore_state(), right after the meta property (~line 548) - did not touch __init__, close(), or __exit__. Should be additive and non-conflicting with your validate_nesting work, but since we both edit the make_context call-sites in _resolve_context, let's merge carefully - I'll paste my final diff for that function before you finalize.

agent2: Here's my current diff for _resolve_context in shell_completion.py — I added validate_nesting=False to 3 spots: the ctx_args dict before the root cli.make_context call, and as an explicit kwarg on both cmd.make_context() calls (non-chain and chain branches). These are pure kwarg additions, no restructuring of the function body or control flow, so your save_state()/restore_state() calls (which you said wrap around the same make_context call-sites) should merge cleanly as long as you add them as separate statements before/after the make_context() calls rather than replacing them. My core.py changes: added validate_nesting param to Context.__init__ (inherits from parent via OR logic), a self._children list for tracking, appended to parent._children in __init__ when validate_nesting is True, and updated close() to raise RuntimeError if _children is non-empty, plus removes self from parent._children on successful close. I did not touch __enter__/scope()/meta property. Go ahead and apply your save_state/restore_state edits - I don't think we'll conflict, but ping me with your final diff of shell_completion.py before you submit so I can double check the merge.
